Personal Loan Interest Rates

Personal Loan Interest Rates is determined based on the applicant’s credit profile and the lenders’ credit risk-based pricing model, cost of funds, etc. As the interest rates on your personal loan primarily influences the total cost of your borrowing, one must always look for best interest rates available on their credit profile.

For this, consumers should first check offers from lenders with whom they already have a deposit, credit card and/or loan account(s). Then, they should visit paisabazaar to compare interest rates offered by top banks and NBFCs.

Personal Loan Fees & Other Charges

While the fees and charges of personal loans usually vary widely depending on lender and the credit profile of the loan applicants, the following table will give you a fair idea of the personal loan fees and charges levied by the lenders:

Particulars Charges
Loan Processing Fees 0.5% to 4% of loan amount
Prepayment/Part-prepayment/Foreclosure Charges For Floating Rate: Nil
For Fixed Rate: Usually around 2% - 5% on the principal outstanding
Loan Cancellation Usually around Rs. 3,000
Stamp Duty Charges As per actuals
Legal Fees As per actuals
Penal Charges Usually @ 2% per month; 24% p.a.
EMI/Cheque Bounce Around Rs. 400 per bounce

Other fees and charges levied by the lenders include documentation charges, verification charges, duplicate statement charges and NOC certificate charges.

Eligibility and Document Requirements for Personal Loan

Personal Loan Eligibility Criteria

Personal loan eligibility criteria can vary from one lender to another due to their varying credit risk policies. The eligibility criteria can also vary as per the loan variant and the occupation profile of the applicant. Here are some of personal loan eligibility criteria used by most lenders:

  • Age: 18 – 60 years
  • Salary: At least Rs 15,000 per month for salaried customers
  • Income: At least Rs 5 lakh p.a. for self-employed customers
  • Credit Score: Preferably 750 and above as having higher credit scores improve chances of loan approval
  • Employment Stability: Usually 1 year with some lenders requiring longer work experience
  • Business Continuity: Usually 3 years of business continuity with some lenders requiring longer period of business continuity
  • Employment Type: Salaried employees working with reputed organizations, MNCs, Private and Public Limited Companies, Govt. organizations, PSUs, and large enterprises

Documents Required for Personal Loan

Personal loan lenders require documents from their applicants to establish their identity, income, loan repayment capacity and place of residence. These documents are more or less the same across lenders. Here are the few common set of documents that you will need to submit when applying for a personal loan:

Requirements Salaried Individuals Self Employed
Proof of Identity Passport, Voter’s ID, Driving License, Aadhaar Card or PAN Card Passport, Voter’s ID, Driving License, Aadhaar Card or PAN Card
Proof of Residence Aadhaar Card, Passport or utility bills Aadhaar Card, Passport or utility bills
Proof of Income Salary Slips, Bank Account Statement, Form 16 Previous Years’ ITR, P&L Statement and Balance Sheet, Bank Account Statement

Compare Personal Loan Offers Before You Apply

The financial requirements of every borrower are different, and there is no single personal loan scheme that meets everyone’s requirements. A practically suitable personal loan offer depends on your loan affordability, including the amount you need, the offered interest rate, applicable repayment tenure, and eventually, the EMI that has to be paid.

Compare multiple loan offers from different lenders provided below to understand how you can manage to get the most affordable EMI at the lowest total interest possible.

Compare Personal Loan Principal Amounts

Your EMI and total repayment amount depend directly on the principal amount you borrow. To keep the overall cost of the loan low, you should borrow only what you need, not the total amount you are eligible for.

Loan Amount Approx. EMI (3 Years @10% p.a.) Common Purpose
₹1 Lakh ₹3,227 Emergency expenses
₹2 Lakh ₹6,454 Medical treatment
₹3 Lakh ₹9,681 Education or travel
₹5 Lakh ₹16,134 Wedding, home renovation or debt consolidation
₹7.5 Lakh ₹24,202 Large planned expenses
₹10 Lakh ₹32,268 High-value financial needs

Expert Tip

Optimise your principal amount smartly while applying for the loan. Borrowing only what you need ensures you pay a lower total interest on your personal loan over the repayment period.

Compare Loan Tenures

Select the most suitable loan tenure that matches your affordability without significantly increasing the overall cost of the loan.

Tenure Monthly EMI* Total Interest Best For
2 years ₹23,072 ₹53,719 Lowest interest cost
3 years ₹16,134 ₹80,809 Balanced repayment
5 years ₹10,624 ₹1,37,411 Lower monthly EMI
7 years ₹8,302 ₹1,97,368 Maximum repayment flexibility

*Illustration based on a ₹5 lakh personal loan at 10% p.a.

Which Tenure should You Choose?

Choose a Shorter Tenure if you:

  • can comfortably afford a higher EMI.
  • want to pay less total interest.
  • prefer becoming debt-free sooner.

Choose a Longer Tenure if you:

  • want a lower monthly EMI.
  • need greater flexibility in your monthly budget.
  • prefer spreading repayments over a longer period.

Compare Interest Rates from Different Lenders

Your total borrowing cost can significantly increase even with a small difference in the interest rate.

Interest Rate Monthly EMI* Total Interest
10% ₹16,134 ₹80,809
11% ₹16,370 ₹89,309
12% ₹16,607 ₹97,858
13% ₹16,847 ₹1,06,491
14% ₹17,089 ₹1,15,197

*Illustration based on a ₹5 lakh personal loan availed for a tenure of 3 years.

Expert Tip

Securing the lowest available interest rate can save you thousands of rupees over the loan tenure. Compare all offers from banks and NBFCs before applying.

Compare EMI vs Total Interest

A lower EMI often comes at the cost of paying more interest over time. Select the most suitable option after considering the following:

Priority Recommended Option
Lowest EMI Longer tenure
Lowest Interest Cost Shorter tenure
Faster Loan Closure Shortest affordable tenure
Balanced Repayment Medium tenure (3–4 years)

Which Personal Loan Option is the Best for You?

If you want to... Consider...
Reduce your monthly EMI Opt for a longer repayment tenure
Save on the total interest cost Choose the shortest affordable tenure
Borrow only what you need Select an appropriate loan amount
Get the best loan offer Compare offers from multiple lenders before applying
Improve approval chances Maintain a high credit score and stable income profile

Important Points To Remember

  • Compare interest rates from multiple lenders before applying.
  • Use the Personal Loan EMI Calculator to estimate your EMI and total repayment before making a decision.
  • Review all applicable charges, including processing fees and prepayment charges, in addition to the interest rate.

Personal Loan EMI Calculator 2026

Use Paisabazaar's personal loan EMI calculator to plan your repayments before you apply. Enter your loan amount, interest rate, and tenure to instantly see your monthly EMI.

EMI Formula Explained

The EMI (Equated Monthly Instalment) for a personal loan is calculated using the reducing balance method:

EMI = [P x R x (1+R)^N] / [(1+R)^N - 1]

Where: P = Principal loan amount | R = Monthly interest rate (Annual rate / 12 / 100) | N = Loan tenure in months

Sample EMI Calculation

Example: You borrow Rs. 10,00,000 at 12% p.a. for 4 years (48 months)

  • Monthly rate R = 12/12/100 = 0.01
  • EMI = [10,00,000 x 0.01 x (1.01)^48] / [(1.01)^48 - 1]
  • EMI = Rs. 26,334 per month
  • Total repayment = Rs. 12,64,032 | Total interest paid = Rs. 2,64,032

Quick EMI Reference Table

Loan Amount 10% p.a. / 3 Yrs 12% p.a. / 4 Yrs 15% p.a. / 5 Yrs
Rs. 2 Lakh Rs. 6,453 Rs. 5,267 Rs. 4,758
Rs. 5 Lakh Rs. 16,133 Rs. 13,167 Rs. 11,895
Rs. 10 Lakh Rs. 32,267 Rs. 26,334 Rs. 23,790
Rs. 20 Lakh Rs. 64,534 Rs. 52,668 Rs. 47,580

Factors Affecting Personal Loan EMI

An Equated Monthly Installment (EMI) is the amount you pay each month. This money goes towards paying back the loan amount and the interest. 3 major factors that influence your personal loan EMIs are:

  • Principal Amount: An increase in the principal amount leads to an increase in your EMI, provided the values for loan tenure and interest rate remain unchanged.
  • Interest Rate: Personal loan EMIs increase with every increment in the interest rates.
  • Tenure: Choosing longer tenure on your loans results in lower EMIs. However, it also leads to higher overall interest cost on the loan.
EMI Calculators
SBI Personal Loan EMI Calculator ICICI Personal Loan EMI Calculator
HDFC Personal Loan EMI Calculator Axis Bank Personal Loan EMI Calculator

Types of Personal Loans in India

Personal loans are offered to borrowers to fulfill their financial requirements for varied purposes. Here is a list of various personal loan types available in India:

Instant Personal Loan

This loan is designed for quick approval and disbursal, often within hours and is best for emergencies. It requires minimal documentation and relies heavily on your credit profile.

Flexi Personal Loan

Flexi loans are an overdraft-style facility where the applicant gets a credit limit approved and can withdraw as needed. Interest is charged only on the amount used. This can be a preferred option for managing irregular expenses.

Pre-approved Personal Loan

These loans are offered to existing bank customers based on their credit and relationship history. They come with pre-set loan amounts and faster processing times and available to select borrowers with good credit profiles.

Top-up Loan

An additional loan amount is offered on top of an existing personal loan to eligible borrowers. These loans are available after a certain repayment history and are useful when you need extra funds without taking a fresh loan.

Personal Loan Balance Transfer

These loans allow you to transfer your existing personal loan to another lender at a lower interest rate to reduce your EMI or total interest burden. These are most effective when done early in your loan tenure.

Personal Loan Balance Transfer Flexi Personal Loan
Short Term Personal Loan Top Up Personal Loan
NRI Personal Loan Loan for Doctors
Personal Loan for Salaried Employees Personal Loan for Self Employed

What can I use a personal loan for?

Personal loans can be availed for any purpose, except for speculation or illegal activities. Borrowers can use the loan proceeds for covering medical emergencies, home renovation, wedding, vacation, business operations, etc. The loan can also be used for managing everyday expenses or for covering higher education costs (including courses not eligible for education loan) and for consolidating multiple high-interest debts.

You can use a personal loan for different financial needs. Here are the most common purposes:

Purpose Best For Typical Amount
Personal Loan for Wedding Marriage expenses, catering, venue Rs. 2L – Rs. 25L
Personal Loan for Medical Hospital bills, surgery, treatment Rs. 50K – Rs. 20L
Travel Loan Domestic or international trips Rs. 50K – Rs. 5L
Loan for Home Renovation Interior work, repairs, furnishing Rs. 1L – Rs. 25L
Personal Loan for Education Short courses, certifications, exams Rs. 50K – Rs. 5L
Debt Consolidation Loan Pay off multiple debts at lower rate Rs. 1L – Rs. 20L

What is the maximum loan amount I can get on my personal loan?

Lenders usually offer personal loans between Rs 5,000 and Rs 50 lakhs. However, the amount that they approve of is primarily dependent on your income and existing loan repayment obligations, if any. Some banks and NBFCs use the Multiplier Method, wherein the eligible loan amount is calculated on the basis of a predetermined multiple (ranging between 10 and 24 times) of an applicant’s net monthly income.

Other lenders use the EMI/NMI Ratio, wherein the EMI of the proposed loan cannot exceed a pre-determined proportion of the applicant. Some lenders also use a combination of both of these methods to find the loan amount eligible of the applicant.

5,000 Personal loan 10,000 Personal Loan
20,000 Personal Loan 50,000 Personal Loan
1 Lakh Personal Loan 5 Lakh Personal Loan
10 Lakh Personal Loan 20 Lakh Personal Loan
40 Lakh Personal Loan 50 Lakh Personal Loan

What is the CIBIL Score Required for a Personal Loan?

Your CIBIL Score is a critical factor that not only determines your loan approval but also the rates and loan terms. Let us understand how CIBIL score impacts loan approval.

CIBIL Score Range Loan Approval Chances Scenario
760 – 900 Very High Best offers available including pre-approved offers
700 – 759 High Most lenders will approve but best offers may not be available
650 – 699 Low Some lenders may approve at higher rates and stricter terms
600 – 649 Very Low Limited options, very high interest rates and stricter terms
Below 600 Difficult Almost all lenders would reject. Need to rebuild credit profile.

Note: The information provided above is only indicative and actual results may vary.

Tip: Check your FREE CIBIL score on Paisabazaar website before apply for personal loan online. This is a soft inquiry and does not affect your credit score.

Will checking my loan offers on Paisabazaar impact my CIBIL score?

No, your credit score is not impacted when checking pre-approved offers and comparing rates on Paisabazaar as it is considered a "soft inquiry" and does not feature in your credit report.

Paisabazaar’s consumer insights report

Paisabazaar’s consumer insights report, “The Personal Loan Story” revealed key insights on how and why Indians choose personal loans. Based on a survey of 2,889 borrowers across 23 cities, the report revealed that while 48% borrowed for essential requirements, 36% borrowed to fund aspirations, and 16% for business investments. Additionally, Tier 3 borrowers are 2.4x more likely to borrow for daily needs than Tier 1 borrowers.

Read the full The Personal Loan Story report by Paisabazaar to gain deeper insights into personal loan borrowing trends, purposes and consumer behaviour across India.

FAQs

What is a Personal Loan?

+-

Personal loan is a short to medium term loan, which consumers can avail to meet both personal and business needs other than speculative purposes. They are usually unsecured in nature as consumers do not need to pledge any collateral or security to avail them.

How can I apply for a personal loan?

+-

Individuals can apply for a personal loan by visiting the nearby branch of their preferred bank or NBFC. They can also apply for the loan through lenders’ official website or through their respective internet banking platform and mobile applications.

What is the interest rate on a personal loan?

+-

Personal loan interest rates offered by leading banks and NBFCs start from 10.49% p.a. depending on the credit profile of their loan applicants. However, some public sector banks may offer lower interest rates to their personal loan applicants.

How can I apply for an instant personal loan online?

+-

Many lenders offer pre-approved personal loans with instant disbursals to their select existing customers having good credit profiles. Thus, consumers should first check for such pre-approved offers with banks and NBFCs with whom they have an existing banking relationship. After checking for pre-approved offers, visit online financial marketplaces to compare personal loan offers from various banks and NBFCs offering quick disbursals, preferably within the same day of making the loan application.

How much CIBIL score is required for a personal loan?

+-

Applicants having credit scores of 750 and above usually have higher chances of availing personal loans. However, many banks and NBFCs offer personal loans to individuals with low CIBIL score/credit score but charge higher interest rates to compensate for the higher credit risk associated with those lenders.

What is the maximum loan duration for a personal loan?

+-

The tenure of a regular personal loan usually ranges from 1 year to 5 years. However, some lenders offer maximum personal loan tenure for 6 to 8 years.

What is the difference between an instant personal loan and a regular personal loan?

+-

Instant personal loan is a personal loan variant, which is usually disbursed within a few minutes of making a loan application. These loans are usually pre-approved in nature and are offered to select existing customers having a good credit profile.

What is the minimum salary required to get a personal loan?

+-

The minimum monthly salary required to avail a personal loan varies from lender to lender. However, for large lenders like private and public sector banks, the minimum income eligibility is Rs 15,000 per month and above.

Can I get a personal loan without a salary slip?

+-

Yes, you can get a personal loan without providing salary slips. You can submit your bank account statement/ a copy of Form 16/ employee certificate from the employer, etc. as proof of income to fulfill the eligibility criteria. However, it is always recommended to confirm the list of required documents with the lender as it may vary from one bank to another.

Can I get a personal loan if I have a home loan?

+-

Yes, you can apply for a personal loan even if you already have a home loan. However, the chances of getting the loan approved will depend on whether you have adequate repayment capacity to repay the proposed loan.

Are there any charges for prepaying a personal loan?

+-

Lenders are free to levy prepayment/foreclosure charges on personal loans availed at fixed interest rates. However, the RBI has barred the lenders from levying these charges on loans availed at floating interest rates.

Can I cancel a personal loan after the loan amount is disbursed?

+-

In some cases, you may cancel a personal loan after disbursal subject to the terms and conditions of the lender. Loan cancellation will lead to cancellation charges and processing fees. Please note that all banks do not allow loan cancellation once the amount is disbursed. However, you can always prepay the loan amount as per the terms and conditions of the bank and save on the interest component.

How much personal loan can I get on a Rs. 40,000 salary?

+-

Some lenders use EMI/NMI ratio while some may use a multiplier method to determine their applicant’s eligible personal loan amount. As these ratios may differ across lenders, the maximum personal loan amount that you can avail on Rs 40,000 salary would also vary. Hence, to know your eligible personal loan amount, you have to contact the lender individually. Alternatively, you can visit online financial marketplaces, which will show eligible personal loan amounts from multiple lenders based on your repayment capacity, interest rate and loan tenure.

Can I get a personal loan if my salary is Rs. 12,000?

+-

The minimum income requirements set for availing personal loans vary across lenders. While the minimum monthly salary requirements set by top lenders usually ranges between Rs. 15,000 and Rs 30,000, some lenders also offer personal loans to applicants having lower monthly income. Hence, individuals having low salary should visit online financial marketplaces to check if they are eligible for any personal loan offers based on their current income.

Do I need to provide any security or collateral to apply for a personal loan?

+-

Traditionally, personal loans are unsecured loans, which implies that the borrower does not need to pledge collateral or security to avail the loan. However, some lenders also offer secured personal loans wherein the borrower needs to provide security or collateral to avail the loan. An applicant should opt for the secured loan facility only when he is ineligible for availing unsecured personal loan or is charged very high interest rates for it.

What are the pros of availing a personal loan?

+-

Personal loan comes with several benefits like no restrictions on end usage (other than speculation), no collateral/security requirements, minimal to no documentation and quick processing.

What are the cons of availing a personal loan?

+-

The interest rates of personal loans are usually higher than its secured alternatives like gold loan, loan against property, etc. Also, its short repayment period/tenure (usually till 5 years) leads to higher EMIs, which in turn reduces the EMI affordability and overall loan amount eligibility of the borrower.

Can I take personal loans from two different banks at the same time?

+-

Yes, you may avail personal loans from two different lenders at the same time, provided the lender of your second personal loan is satisfied with your repayment capacity. However, it is better to take a single loan of a larger amount from the lender providing lower interest rates and/or better terms and conditions.

Can a student apply for a personal loan?

+-

Students can apply for personal loans only if they satisfy the various eligibility requirements related to age, income, credit score, etc. set by the lenders.

How do I make overdue payments of my personal loan?

+-

You can make personal loan EMI overdue payments online through the bank’s internet banking platform, mobile application, UPI app or other digital payment systems such as NEFT/RTGS, digital wallets (e-Wallets) and payments apps. You can also visit the lender’s branch to make overdue payments through offline modes like cash, cheque and demand draft (DD).

Can a pensioner get a personal loan?

+-

Lenders offer personal loans to pensioners in the form of pension loans. However, these loans are offered only to those pensioners who maintain their pension account with the respective bank.

Where can I get my no-dues certificate from?

+-

Your existing personal loan lender will provide you with a no-dues certificate after the complete repayment of your personal loan.
